citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
Review of IACUC records, in addition to interviews with facility personnel and IACUC members, identified an instance
where laboratory staff implemented significant changes to an ongoing protocol without prior review and approval by the
IACUC. In November 2025, pigs undergoing a procedure in a toxicology study were to be monitored and given pain
medication every 8-12 hours post procedure by laboratory personnel. However, records show that the administration of
pain medication did not always occur at the frequency stated in the protocol. Specifically, laboratory personnel exceeded
the 12-hour maximum stated in the protocol for 8 pigs during the study reportedly to prevent changes in behavior during
observations. There was no indication of increased pain or discomfort noted by laboratory personnel for these 8 pigs and
they received the pain medication twice daily as stated in the protocol, but the protocol was not followed as written and
approved by the IACUC.
Performing activities not covered by IACUC-approved protocols can result in harm or death to the impacted animals. The
regulations state that with respect to activities involving animals, the IACUC, as an agent of the research facility, shall
review and approve, require modifications in (to secure approval), or withhold approval of proposed significant changes
regarding the care and use of animals in ongoing activities.
This incident was identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented. To remain
corrected after April 7, 2026

On June 25, 2025, an adverse event occurred in two dogs while undergoing a procedure for an approved protocol. The
activity required administration of a test article either orally or intravenously. Two dogs received the test article
intravenously and developed allergic reactions progressing to anaphylaxis. Both dogs were immediately treated by
veterinary staff and recovered. An internal investigation determined that inexperienced laboratory personnel inadvertently
administered the oral test article intravenously which resulted in the allergic reactions in both dogs. Failure to adequately
train laboratory personnel may result in harm or death to animals in their care.
It is the responsibility of the research facility to ensure that all personnel involved in animal use are adequately trained to
perform their duties. Issues associated with staff training can affect animal health. The facility shall ensure that all
personnel are sufficiently trained to conduct their duties.
Corrected prior to the inspection on April 7, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(7) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
Review of IACUC records, in addition to interviews with facility personnel and IACUC members, identified an instance 
where laboratory staff implemented significant changes to an ongoing protocol without prior review and approval by the 
IACUC. In November 2025, pigs undergoing a procedure in a toxicology study were to be monitored and given pain 
medication every 8-12 hours post procedure by laboratory personnel. However, records show that the administration of 
pain medication did not always occur at the frequency stated in the protocol. Specifically, laboratory personnel exceeded 
the 12-hour maximum stated in the protocol for 8 pigs during the study reportedly to prevent changes in behavior during 
observations. There was no indication of increased pain or discomfort noted by laboratory personnel for these 8 pigs and 
they received the pain medication twice daily as stated in the protocol, but the protocol was not followed as written and 
approved by the IACUC.  
 
Performing activities not covered by IACUC-approved protocols can result in harm or death to the impacted animals. The 
regulations state that with respect to activities involving animals, the IACUC, as an agent of the research facility, shall 
review and approve, require modifications in (to secure approval), or withhold approval of proposed significant changes 
regarding the care and use of animals in ongoing activities. 
 
This incident was identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented. To remain 
corrected after April 7, 2026 
2.32(c) 
   
Personnel qualifications. 
On June 25, 2025, an adverse event occurred in two dogs while undergoing a procedure for an approved protocol. The 
activity required administration of a test article either orally or intravenously. Two dogs received the test article 
intravenously and developed allergic reactions progressing to anaphylaxis. Both dogs were immediately treated by 
veterinary staff and recovered. An internal investigation determined that inexperienced laboratory personnel inadvertently 
administered the oral test article intravenously which resulted in the allergic reactions in both dogs. Failure to adequately 
train laboratory personnel may result in harm or death to animals in their care.

It is the responsibility of the research facility to ensure that all personnel involved in animal use are adequately trained to 
perform their duties. Issues associated with staff training can affect animal health. The facility shall ensure that all 
personnel are sufficiently trained to conduct their duties. 
 
Corrected prior to the inspection on April 7, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
